Honey Lee Cottrell papers
Cottrell, Honey Lee.
S.I.R. Productions
7822
4.1 cubic feet. 4.1 cubic feet.
1950-2015. Photographs, artwork, and printed materials. Personal papers and photographic archives. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 13 ======= 13 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 9186484
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

NOTES
Collecting program: Human Sexuality Collection.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Photographs
Series Ia. Erotic Portraits Series Ib. On Our Backs photographs Series Ic. Personal Photographs Series Id. General Photographs Series Ie. Famous Lesbians Series If. Slides
Series II. Paper Documents
Series III. Publications
Series IV. Interviews
SEPARATED MATERIAL
Books - cataloged individually in the Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
The Blatant Image: A Magazine of Feminist Photography, no. 2, 1982 ( with 2 images by Honey Lee Cottrell and a note by her on the inside cover. Self-portrait on p. 16 and her portrait of Jeanette Foster on p. 30.)
Gayle, Marilyn What Lesbians Do, 1975 (inscribed and signed by the author and artist Barbary)
Cite As:
Honey Lee Cottrell Papers, #7822. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Existence and Location of Copies
Digital scans of some photographs are also available through the digital portal.
Biographical / Historical
Honey Lee Cottrell (1946-2015) was a noted photographer who helped define lesbian erotica in the 1980s through her contributions to the women's sex magazine On Our Backs over its first seven years. She studied at the National Sex Forum and was a volunteer switchboard operator at San Francisco Sex Information. Her photographs appeared in I Am My Lover (1979), Coming to Power (1981), A Dykes Bike Repair Handbook (1990), and Nothing But the Girl (1996), as well as in magazines including The Blatant Image and Sinister Wisdom. In 1981, Cottrell received a BA in film studies from San Francisco State University. She was director and camera for Sweet Dreams starring Pat Califia (National Sex Forum, 1980), and from 1985 to the early 90s, a cinematographer for Fatale Video, the first lesbian-created erotic movie company. In the late 1980s and early 1990s, she also collaborated on erotic and sex education videos of interest to lesbians and others produced by S.I.R. (Sex Indulgence & Rock.n Roll), a video production company run by Shar Rednour and Jackie Strano. She took photos of Susie Bright and Shar Rednour for their book covers. She photographed her lovers and friends and documented queer and kink cultures for decades with her first camera, a 35 mm Nikkormat.
Scope and content
The Honey Lee Cottrell papers document the personal life and professional work of photographer Honey Lee Cottrell, dating 2950-2015. The collection includes many test prints, notes about how to print particular images, items collected for collage projects, event publicity flyers, and files of collaboration with Tee Corinne, Susie Bright, Joani Blank, Shar Rednour, Jackie Strano, Phyllis Christopher (friend and staff photographer for the periodical On Our Backs), and others. Also included are 19 black and white photos by Honey Lee Cottrell, 4 strips of contact prints, and a Tee Corinne image possibly printed by Cottrell. Subjects include Seeley, lesbians on California whitewater rafting trips organized by Seeley, Maxine Feldman (San Francisco, March 1977), and 2 images from Seeley and Cottrell's trip to Europe. Also, 4 color photographs by Marsha Seeley of Cottrell and Maxine Feldman, San Francisco, March 1977.
Women-owned business enterprises. Women -- Sexual behavior. Sex oriented businesses. Sex instruction. Pornography. Pornographic films. Photography of women. Photography, Erotic. Lesbians -- Pictorial works. Feminism. Erotica. Butch and femme (Lesbian culture). On our backs. Bright, Susie, 1958- Califia, Patrick, 1954- Christopher, Phyllis, 1963- Corinne, Tee, 1943-2006. Hollibaugh, Amber L., 1946- Kinney, Nan. Rubin, Gayle. Sundahl, Deborah. Williams, Rachael

Rachael Williams, a Black dyke who in 1981 became the first Ms. Leather and the first Black person to hold the equivalent of a national title.

Scope and Contents
Two copies, one with signatures and notesOn Our BacksJanuary-JuneNovember 1989329On Our BacksJanuary-October1990330On Our BacksJanuary 1992September 1994331On Our Backs calendar1989331Series IV. InterviewsInterview of Honey Lee Cottrell by Brenda MarstonSeptember 6, 2015
Scope and Contents
Description of full recording: On Sunday, September 6, 2015, Honey Lee Cottrell and Brenda Marston spent a few hours looking through Cottrell's files and photographs, which she is sending to be preserved in Cornell's Human Sexuality Collection. Cottrell explained the organization and highlights of the collection. A video camera was left on to record the conversation, which could contain helpful insight to those who later want to use the collection. At the end, Cottrell says "They need to be turned loose. I'm glad we're turning them loose on Cornell, but I'm like, man! You know, who's going to be lining up at Cornell to see these things?"
An excerpt of the interview can be found here:
Description of Excerpt: September 6, 2015. Talking about her vision for the first issue of On Our Backs and the psycho-sexual subway map of urban lesbian life she created. Shows an ad for her erotic photography and reflects on the vision for both On Our Backs and Countervision. Highlights of her commentary as we look at the photographs and files she is sending to be preserved in Cornell's Human Sexuality Collection. (Talking with Brenda Marston.) 13:54 minutes.Series V. Rachael Williams, a Black dyke who in 1981 became the first Ms. Leather and the first Black person to hold the equivalent of a national title.

Scope and Contents
Two copies, one with signatures and notesOn Our BacksJanuary-JuneNovember 1989329On Our BacksJanuary-October1990330On Our BacksJanuary 1992September 1994331On Our Backs calendar1989331Series IV. InterviewsInterview of Honey Lee Cottrell by Brenda MarstonSeptember 6, 2015
Scope and Contents
Description of full recording: On Sunday, September 6, 2015, Honey Lee Cottrell and Brenda Marston spent a few hours looking through Cottrell's files and photographs, which she is sending to be preserved in Cornell's Human Sexuality Collection. Cottrell explained the organization and highlights of the collection. A video camera was left on to record the conversation, which could contain helpful insight to those who later want to use the collection. At the end, Cottrell says "They need to be turned loose. I'm glad we're turning them loose on Cornell, but I'm like, man! You know, who's going to be lining up at Cornell to see these things?"
An excerpt of the interview can be found here:
Description of Excerpt: September 6, 2015. Talking about her vision for the first issue of On Our Backs and the psycho-sexual subway map of urban lesbian life she created. Shows an ad for her erotic photography and reflects on the vision for both On Our Backs and Countervision. Highlights of her commentary as we look at the photographs and files she is sending to be preserved in Cornell's Human Sexuality Collection. (Talking with Brenda Marston.) 13:54 minutes.Series V.
